Shortlist may 29 4.20.26 KK - fu on deliverables 4.13.26 KK - had followup deliverables submitted april 10th 3.30.26 KK - timeline updated, RFP due April 3 3.13.26 KK - open questions shared - responses back by march 27 3.2.26 KK -recevied RFP, aligning internally on details and gaps 2.23.26 KK - RFP planning to be released on Friday 2.27. Requested presales
hard.ais_why:
• The description and next-step notes reveal a heavily structured RFP: send RFP, Q&A, top-3 selection, vendor selection July 31. The buyer's stated requirements are a centralized cloud-based solution that enforces structured workflows and is ERP-agnostic. There is zero mention of AI, Merlin, Agentic, GenAI, intake or autonomous capabilities in the buyer-stated requirements.
• AI importance is rated 'Don't Know' - the strongest negative indicator. Zycus has no read on buyer AI appetite after multiple RFP cycles. An ERP transition over three years is the dominant driver, not AI. Products include Merlin, Merlin Intake and ANA, but no Agentic AI; the Merlin product line lists ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake. This is vendor-side scoping with no buyer pull.
• Avoma was fully exhausted - zero calls at opportunity, account or copaair.com domain level despite 10 contacts on file. The process is being run entirely through the RFP document with no recorded vendor calls. With a 'Don't Know' AI rating, an ERP-transition-driven RFP scope and zero AI voice across the detailed timeline, this is Resistant.

north_star_verdict.headline: Copa froze the entire S2P initiative on 01 Jun 2026 (Myron Isaacs: all new initiatives on hold until the war ends and oil prices drop) with no restart date. Last buyer touch was 35 days ago, there is no champion, no engaged economic buyer, and zero recorded voice calls across the whole Feb-Jun RFP. The 01 Dec close date is not credible and the Pipeline forecast should be omitted until a written restart trigger exists.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Feb 2026, qualified 17 Feb (4 days), Formal Evaluation since 02 Mar (126 days in stage, no progression since the 01 Jun freeze). Original RFP plan: shortlist 29 May (never happened), vendor selection 31 Jul, close 01 Dec. Days-to-close from 06 Jul: 148. Amount tripled 01 Apr ($120k to $358.9k) with no matching stage advance or buyer validation. Verdict trajectory: holding steady at Off Track versus the prior sweep, with the buyer-imposed freeze still the single blocker most likely to prevent the close.
evidence: Buyer freeze, 01 Jun 2026: 'To be perfectly honest, all new initiatives are on hold until the war ends and oil prices drop to reasonable levels. I'll keep you posted once we are in a position to acquire the tool.' (Myron Isaacs, Negotiations Specialist, Purchasing; SF Next_Step__c and Task 01 Jun 2026).
The 29 May 2026 vendor shortlist the buyer promised never issued; on 01 Jun the rep chased it and received the freeze message instead.
Zero OpportunityContactRole records: no champion, economic buyer, or decision maker mapped; only procurement contacts Myron Isaacs and Alejandro Rovira appear across the entire RFP.
Zero Avoma calls found via opportunity-id, account-id, or copaair.com attendee-email discovery; the whole RFP ran over email with no recorded voice engagement.
AIS_Status Negative (score 3): buyer requirements carry no AI/Merlin/Agentic language and AI importance is rated 'Don't Know'.
Amount tripled from $120k to $358.9k on 01 Apr 2026 with no stage progression or buyer validation of the larger scope.
Pipeline forecast with 148 days to close, a buyer-imposed indefinite freeze, no champion and no economic buyer is indefensible.

metrics.narrative: Copa's stated driver is operational, not quantified: they are moving from manual, fragmented procurement to a centralized, cloud-based, ERP-agnostic S2P suite ahead of a planned ERP transition within three years (SF Description and the 03 Apr 2026 RFP-response email). No cost-savings target, cycle-time goal, error-rate baseline, or ROI figure was ever provided by the buyer, and the synthesis-map value fields carry no quantified case. The one hard financial signal is external, not internal: on 01 Jun 2026 Myron Isaacs tied the freeze to war and oil prices, confirming cost control now dominates. Quantifying procurement inefficiency and framing Zycus as a cost-reduction lever is the move that would give this deal a defendable anchor to survive the freeze.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. Every touch runs through Myron Isaacs, a Negotiations Specialist in Purchasing with no budget authority, who merely relayed the freeze rather than owning it (Task 01 Jun 2026). The freeze decision, halting all new initiatives on macro grounds, was clearly taken above Myron by a finance or executive sponsor who has never appeared in any email, call, or contact role. What the evidence shows about budget authority is only that it sits with an unnamed cost-control owner reacting to oil prices. Identifying and reaching that finance sponsor is a ranked move; without it Zycus cannot influence restart timing or budget reallocation.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are documented through the RFP itself: a centralized, cloud-based S2P suite that enforces structured procurement workflows, is ERP-agnostic to support a future ERP migration, and integrates with SAP (SF Description). On 04 Apr 2026 Copa confirmed receipt of 25 Zycus files, opened proposal review, and asked for module-by-module comparison against their prior Coupa deployment plus a specific 5,000-user shopping-cart use case, showing functional fit and price-comparability are live criteria. These are confirmed from the RFP and email trail rather than a discovery call, since no calls exist. AI capability is explicitly not a criterion (AIS 'Don't Know').
sources: Task 04 Apr 2026 (25 files, module comparison, shopping-cart use case)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, document-driven multi-vendor RFP: issue RFP, Q&A round (questions shared 13 Mar, answers by 27 Mar), deliverables submitted 10 Apr, top-3 shortlist targeted 29 May, vendor selection planned 31 Jul (SF Next_Step_History__c and the email trail). The process is procurement-owned and gated through the RFP portal, with no evidence of a business or executive review stage yet. That process is now suspended: the 29 May shortlist never issued and the 01 Jun freeze paused vendor selection indefinitely. The approval chain above procurement is unconfirmed, which is the key process gap.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been reached: the deal sits in Formal Evaluation with no MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal, or InfoSec activity, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated as a forcing function. This is expected at this stage, but the freeze means paper is far off. Copa's 04 Apr request that Zycus quote on their standard order-form format (not the Excel template) is the only paper-adjacent signal, indicating a defined procurement channel. Visibility here will only come once the evaluation restarts and commercials open; there is nothing to progress until then.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a manual, fragmented procurement operation that Copa wants to standardize on a governed, cloud-based S2P platform with better visibility and control ahead of an ERP transition (SF Description; Zycus 03 Apr RFP response acknowledging the move 'from manual, fragmented' processes). The pain owner is Purchasing, voiced by Myron Isaacs. An overlaid and now-dominant pain is inferred: acute cost pressure from the fuel-price and macro environment, which the buyer stated as the reason all new initiatives are frozen (01 Jun 2026). The procurement-efficiency pain was never quantified, so it is currently outweighed by the cash-preservation pain.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Across the full Feb-Jun 2026 RFP only two procurement contacts engaged: Myron Isaacs (Negotiations Specialist, Purchasing), who ran Q&A, acknowledged deliverables ('Information well received', 09 Apr 2026) and relayed the freeze, and Alejandro Rovira, who handled RFP coordination. Neither introduced Zycus to a finance or executive sponsor, shared competitive intel, coached positioning, or advocated when the freeze hit, and there is no OpportunityContactRole or Avoma call to suggest advocacy. The finance sponsor who ordered the freeze never appeared. Building a genuine champion inside Copa procurement is the prerequisite move before any commercial step.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the only named competitive reference: on 04 Apr 2026 Copa disclosed they were subscribed to Coupa (100 users) in 2019, attached the installed modules, and asked Zycus for an equivalent quote 'for our comparison purposes', making Coupa the pricing and functional benchmark. This is a formal, portal-run multi-vendor RFP (top-3 shortlist planned), so other Tier-1 S2P vendors are almost certainly in the field, but none are named in any Salesforce field, task, or next step and there are zero calls to surface the shortlist, so their identity and Zycus's relative position are genuinely unknown. How we win: land a module-for-module price and fit comparison that beats the buyer's Coupa reference, then reframe the suite as cost-reduction during the downturn to survive the freeze.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is negative. After a fast qualification (Qualified 17 Feb, Formal Evaluation 02 Mar) the deal advanced only on paper: amount tripled 01 Apr while stage never moved, the promised 29 May shortlist never issued, and the buyer imposed an indefinite freeze on 01 Jun. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange: the 01 Jun freeze email. Every Next_Step edit since (16 Jun, 29 Jun) is a rep-side 'same as below' status note, not buyer engagement.

items.change: Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation
Amount tripled from $120k to $358.9k with no stage advance
Copa opened review of Zycus's 25-file proposal and requested Coupa-comparison quote and a 5,000-user shopping-cart use case
Buyer froze all new initiatives indefinitely citing war and oil prices; no restart date

competitive_position.summary: The single named competitive reference is Coupa: on 04 Apr 2026 Copa cited their 2019 Coupa deployment (100 users) and asked Zycus to quote against it for comparison, making Coupa the current pricing benchmark rather than a confirmed active rival. This is a top-3 multi-vendor RFP, so other Tier-1 S2P vendors are very likely present, but the evidence names none and there are zero calls to reveal the shortlist, so the wider field is a genuine knowledge gap rather than an inferred name. The overriding competitive threat right now is not a vendor at all but the buyer-imposed freeze (do-nothing / project-pause), which halts the entire evaluation.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: On 2019 we were subscribed to Coupa (100 users), attached you will find the modules installed, please send us your equivalent quote (use your standard order form format, not excel template) for our comparison purposes.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a module-for-module price and capability comparison that beats Copa's Coupa reference, and pair it with an ERP-agnostic transition story SAP-native rivals cannot match.

competitors.name: Do nothing (macro freeze)
competitors.quote: To be perfectly honest, all new initiatives are on hold until the war ends and oil prices drop to reasonable levels.
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe Zycus as a near-term cost-reduction lever during the fuel-price squeeze, not discretionary new spend, to justify restarting inside a cost freeze.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Copa's stated criteria (centralized cloud S2P, structured workflows, ERP-agnostic, SAP integration) map well to the Zycus suite; the open question is proving specific use cases and price parity versus their Coupa reference, none of which can advance while frozen.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a frozen, single-threaded, championless RFP with no voice engagement. The highest-impact levers are re-framing the value around cost, breaking single-thread to the finance sponsor, and building a real champion.
flags: Reframe Zycus as a cost-reduction lever tied to the fuel-price squeeze, not new discretionary spend, to justify restarting inside the freeze.
Break single-thread: the finance sponsor who ordered the 01 Jun freeze has never engaged; reach them through Myron or Copa procurement leadership.
Build a genuine champion in Copa Purchasing; five months of RFP with only transactional contacts and zero calls leaves no internal advocate.

items.name: Myron Isaacs
items.title: Negotiations Specialist, Purchasing
items.risk: Single point of contact for the entire RFP. Transactional: issued the RFP, ran Q&A, acknowledged deliverables, and relayed the freeze. No budget authority, no advocacy, no executive access.
items.source: Tasks 04 Apr / 09 Apr / 01 Jun 2026 (email signature: 'Negotiations Specialist | Purchasing | Copa Airlines')

items.name: Alejandro Rovira
items.risk: RFP coordination / administrative contact. No decision authority or coaching behaviour; last visible touch 13 Mar.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Only two procurement contacts (Myron Isaacs, Alejandro Rovira) engaged across the five-month RFP, neither showing advocacy, executive access, or coaching, and neither resisted the freeze. Zycus has no internal partner to influence restart timing.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (zero records); Tasks Feb-Jun 2026; Avoma (zero calls)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is absent from the buyer's requirements and Zycus has no read on AI appetite (AIS 'Don't Know', Negative). Merlin/ANA are in the product scope but purely vendor-side; the buyer's RFP is driven by workflow standardization and an ERP transition, so the agentic story is not landing and is under-positioned.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 3, AIS_Status Negative. Buyer requirements mention zero AI/Merlin/Agentic/GenAI language; AI importance rated 'Don't Know'. No calls exist to test whether the field under- or over-rates true appetite, so the Negative read stands.

items.detail: Buyer froze all new initiatives indefinitely citing war and oil prices; no restart date and cash preservation now overrides the project.
No economic buyer or finance sponsor mapped; the freeze decision was made above the only contact, leaving Zycus with no access to power.
No champion after five months; single-thread into procurement with no advocacy to restart or accelerate.
Coupa is the buyer's stated pricing and module benchmark from their 2019 deployment; Zycus must win a module-for-module comparison to differentiate.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling to cold. Buyer went silent after the 01 Jun freeze; every subsequent Next_Step edit is a rep-side status note. No voice engagement at any point.

Zero Avoma calls across all discovery methods

scope_change.detail: Amount tripled on 01 Apr 2026 to the full S2P suite plus Merlin/ANA, but the scope grew on paper only with no stage advance or buyer validation, so it is unconfirmed expansion rather than momentum.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Copa's buyer imposed an indefinite freeze on the entire S2P initiative on 01 Jun 2026 (war and oil prices) on a single-threaded, championless RFP with no economic buyer and no recorded calls; the only thing between us and a dead deal is a written restart trigger, and the 01 Dec close date is unreachable.

win_position.text: We are a shortlisted RFP vendor whose proposal is under review, but Copa froze the whole S2P initiative on 01 Jun with no restart date and we hold no champion or economic-buyer access to reopen it.
Every touch runs through Myron Isaacs, a Negotiations Specialist with no budget authority; the finance sponsor who ordered the freeze has never engaged.
AI excitement is Negative (AIS 3) and Coupa is the buyer's stated pricing benchmark from their 2019 deployment, so Zycus lacks a differentiated wedge.

deal_momentum.text: Zero forward movement since the 02 Mar stage change; every Next Step since is a rep-side status note and the buyer went silent after the 01 Jun freeze.
The 29 May shortlist the buyer promised never issued and vendor selection (planned 31 Jul) is now indefinitely paused.

customer_commitment.text: No Avoma calls across opportunity, account, or copaair.com attendee discovery; the entire Feb-Jun RFP ran over email with no voice engagement.
The amount tripled to $358.9k on 01 Apr on paper only, with no stage advance or buyer validation of the larger scope.

deal_risk.text: Buyer halted all new initiatives 'until the war ends and oil prices drop' (Myron Isaacs, 01 Jun) with no committed restart, a no-decision / project-pause risk rather than a competitive loss.
No champion, no economic buyer, and single-thread into procurement leave Zycus with no internal advocate to force a restart.

deal_momentum.evidence: Indefinite buyer freeze 01 Jun; missed 29 May shortlist; no buyer touch in 35 days.

customer_commitment.evidence: Zero calls, email-only RFP, no champion or EB, paper-only amount increase.

deal_risk.evidence: Macro-driven indefinite freeze with no restart date; close date unreachable.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 52; competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 12 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
